origin.name: Vietnam
origin.death_registration: Local civil registry (Uy ban nhan dan)
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Unexpected, violent, road accident, or suspicious deaths
origin.death_certificate_language: Vietnamese only. Certified English translation required.
origin.road_accident_note: Road accidents are the leading cause of British and Irish tourist deaths in Vietnam. Police investigation adds time.
origin.two_consulate_note: British Embassy in Hanoi covers north Vietnam. Consulate General in Ho Chi Minh City covers south Vietnam. Contact the correct office.
origin.key_document: Giay chung tu (Vietnamese death certificate) with certified English translation
origin.source: FCDO Vietnam travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Hanoi guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Giay chung tu (Vietnamese death certificate) with certified English translation

airlines.name: Vietnam Airlines
airlines.routes: HAN, SGN to LHR (via AMS or CDG)

special_rules: Death certificate is in Vietnamese only. Certified English translation is mandatory.
Road accidents trigger police investigation. Police report must be obtained before process can begin.
British Embassy in Hanoi covers north Vietnam. Consulate General in HCMC covers south.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Vietnamese documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. British Embassy Hanoi (north) or Consulate General Ho Chi Minh City (south).
destinations.seo_title: Vietnam to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Vietnam. Repatriation to the UK takes 14-21 days. Vietnamese documentation and cargo flights from Hanoi or HCMC handled. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
key_documents: Giay chung tu with certified English translation
special_rules: Irish consular matters for Vietnam are handled via the Irish Embassy in Bangkok, Thailand.
Certified English translation of giay chung tu required identically to UK repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Vietnamese documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. Irish consular matters for Vietnam handled via the Irish Embassy in Bangkok.
destinations.seo_title: Vietnam to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Vietnam and needs to come home to Ireland. Repatriation takes 14-21 days. Vietnamese documentation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
